title: TraceWhy full diagnostic casebook
description: Six native Linux comparisons demonstrate every TraceWhy v1 diagnostic capability with linked trace evidence.

cases.label: Missing configuration
cases.description: A worker opens the same configuration path successfully in the good run and receives ENOENT in the bad run.

findings.title: File or directory is missing: worker.toml
findings.summary: openat succeeds for `$PROJECT/worker.toml` in the good run but returns ENOENT in the bad run.
reasons: The operation succeeds in the good run and fails in the bad run.
The resource is referenced in captured error output.

findings.title: Child process p2 exits differently
findings.summary: The good child exits with code 0; the bad child exits with code 78.
reasons: An aligned child process exits differently between runs.
The difference is attached directly to the failing child.

cases.label: Permission denied
cases.description: The same file read changes from success to EACCES after its mode removes access.

findings.title: Permission blocks access to locked.txt
findings.summary: openat succeeds for `$PROJECT/demos/permission-denied/locked.txt` in the good run but returns EACCES in the bad run.
reasons: The operation succeeds in the good run and fails in the bad run.
The resource is referenced in captured error output.

cases.label: Wrong executable on PATH
cases.description: Different PATH values resolve the same command name to different executable files and behavior.

findings.title: Different executable selected for tracewhy-demo-tool
findings.summary: The good run executes `$PROJECT/demos/wrong-executable/bin-good/tracewhy-demo-tool`, while the bad run executes `$PROJECT/demos/wrong-executable/bin-bad/tracewhy-demo-tool`.
reasons: Aligned process executions resolve to different executable paths.
The divergence occurs in process execution, before later child behavior.

findings.title: Environment variable differs: PATH
findings.summary: `PATH` differs between the good and bad environments and can affect command or resource resolution.
reasons: The variable is on TraceWhy's resolution-relevant allowlist.

cases.label: Wrong working directory
cases.description: A relative configuration read resolves from two directories and fails only in the bad run.

findings.title: A relative path resolves from different directories
findings.summary: The good run starts in `/workspace/demos/wrong-working-directory/good` and the bad run starts in `/workspace/demos/wrong-working-directory/bad`.
reasons: Relative files and executable lookups resolve from the working directory.
The same relative operation succeeds in the good run and fails in the bad run.

cases.label: Child process failure
cases.description: The aligned child process changes exit status and TraceWhy attaches the failure to that process.

findings.title: Child process p2 exits differently
findings.summary: The good child exits with code 0; the bad child exits with code 7.
reasons: An aligned child process exits differently between runs.
The difference is attached directly to the failing child.

cases.label: Shared library unavailable
cases.description: The dynamic loader finds libdemo.so in the good environment and cannot load it in the bad environment.

findings.title: Shared library differs: libdemo.so
findings.summary: The good run loads `$PROJECT/demos/shared-library/good/libdemo.so`, while the bad loader returns ENOENT for `$PROJECT/demos/shared-library/bad/libdemo.so`.
reasons: The library loads in the good run and is missing in the bad run.
The library is referenced in captured error output.

findings.title: Environment variable differs: LD_LIBRARY_PATH
findings.summary: `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` differs between the good and bad environments and can affect command or resource resolution.
reasons: The variable is on TraceWhy's resolution-relevant allowlist.
